visualGoal: Make a bundle owner internalize the buy ORDER (bundle, pedals, rig, base, taste) as a visual ladder before they hit the torque-before-pedals warning, and steer them toward the load-cell brake as the first upgrade. Real product proof at every rung of the ladder; torque (the base) shown fourth, not first. No procedural art.

realProductPhotosNeeded.notes: Referenced in Stage 1 as the PC-only cheapest-DD alternative (text + Amazon link + tip callout only). No verified image, so it is intentionally NOT embedded as a gearpick/shotgrid. Sourcing a real photo would let it join the Stage 1 visuals; not a blocker for this article since MOZA R3 (verified) already anchors Stage 1.
Named in Stage 4 as a 12Nm (15Nm-firmware) alternative to the MOZA R12 (text mention only). No verified image, so text-only. Not a blocker; the verified MOZA R12 anchors Stage 4.

editorialCompositePrompts.prompt: Realistic Gen X garage sim-racing bench at golden-hour rim light, a staged upgrade progression laid left-to-right on a workbench — an entry DD bundle box, a set of load-cell pedals, an 8020 aluminum-profile rig frame section, and a mid-tier wheelbase on an open 4-bolt mount — with open product-overlay footprints beside each stage for real product photos, curator Duke Alvarez gesturing along the row like a ladder, cable tray visible, no fake branded hardware, no fake UI, 16:9 editorial cover.
editorialCompositePrompts.negativePrompt: fake logos, fake wheelbase, warped controls, impossible ergonomics, tiny unreadable text, procedural gradient art

infographicPrompts.title: The smart upgrade ladder (buy in this order)
content: 2 Load-cell pedals — the biggest consistency gain, lowest spend ($150-400)
3 8020 rig — kill the flex before the base ($450-850 frame)
4 Wheelbase — 9-12Nm, ONLY once you hit the ceiling ($399-699)
5 Taste — haptics / VR / active pedals (enjoyment, not lap time)
infographicPrompts.style: IgnitionSim command-center numbered ladder rising left-to-right, torque shown at rung 4 not rung 1, racing colors, real product-photo placeholders as rung markers for Codex overlay

infographicPrompts.title: Why torque-first wastes money
content: WRONG: bundle -> bigger base (lap times don't move, brake still wanders)
RIGHT: bundle -> load-cell brake (pressure not travel, consistency jumps)
Pedals run over USB — they survive every future base
A base only helps once you can name the car you hit the torque ceiling in
infographicPrompts.style: Two-lane right/wrong comparison card (green/red), MOZA CRP2 photo placeholder on the RIGHT lane for Codex overlay

curatorScenePrompts.prompt: Realistic adult Gen X sim-racing curator Duke Alvarez in a clean garage cockpit bay, one hand resting on a load-cell brake pedal, gesturing 'this first' toward the pedals while a wheelbase sits behind him, confident mechanic energy, product-overlay space beside him, practical not fantasy, 4:5 portrait.
